mako.raw:
{"query":"UNRWA West Bank facilities Israeli allegations terrorism independent investigation findings 2025 official response","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1801,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.un.org/unispal/document/unrwa-humanitarian-response-in-the-occupied-palestinian-territory-flash-appeal-2025-annual-report-summary/","title":"UNRWA Humanitarian response in the occupied Palestinian territory - Flash appeal 2025 annual report summary - Question of Palestine","content":"UNRWA Humanitarian response in the occupied Palestinian territory – Flash appeal 2025 annual report summary
Despite unprecedented operational constraints,1 the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East (UNRWA) remained the linchpin of the humanitarian response in the Occupied Palestinian Territory (OPT) throughout 2025 in both the Gaza Strip and the West Bank, including East Jerusalem.
The 11-week blockade on humanitarian assistance imposed by Israeli authorities, combined with the breakdown of the January ceasefire in March, further disrupted operations and exacerbated the humanitarian crisis.
Approximately 11,500 Palestinian UNRWA personnel in the Gaza Strip, many themselves displaced and affected by hunger and insecurity, continued to work under extreme conditions.
More than 390 colleagues had been killed, including in the line of duty, and over 300 Agency installations were damaged or destroyed.
Following the launch of Operation “Iron Wall” by Israeli forces in January 2025, up to 40,000 people were forcibly displaced from the camps in Jenin, Tulkarm and Nur Shams.
Heightened movement and access restrictions imposed by Israeli authorities deepened socioeconomic vulnerabilities and disrupted access to education, healthcare and shelter.
In response to mass displacement and the humanitarian consequences of escalating violence, UNRWA ― through more than 4,000 Palestinian personnel on the ground ― continued to support coordination and directly deliver life-saving and essential assistance in the occupied West Bank, including East Jerusalem."}]}
